chapter_title.en: Chapter 92 – The perfume for the one in Ihraam

text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنِّي أَكَلْتُ خَبِيصاً حَتَّى شَبِعْتُ وَ أَنَا مُحْرِمٌ فَقَالَ إِذَا فَرَغْتَ مِنْ مَنَاسِكِكَ وَ أَرَدْتَ الْخُرُوجَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ فَابْتَعْ بِدِرْهَمٍ تَمْراً فَتَصَدَّقْ بِهِ فَيَكُونَ كَفَّارَةً لِذَلِكَ وَ لِمَا دَخَلَ فِي إِحْرَامِكَ مِمَّا لَا تَعْلَمُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Abdul Kareem, from Al Hassan Bin Haroun who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘I ate Khabeys (a porridge) until I was satiated, and I am in Ihraam’. So heasws said: ‘When you are free from performing your rituals and intend to exit from Makkah, so buy some dates with one Dirham and give in charity with it, so it would become an expiation for that, and for what might have entered in your Ihraam from what you do not know’.
en.sarwar: 9. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Isma‘il ibn Hanan ibn Sadir from his father who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is in the state of Ihram and uses a salt which is mixed with saffron. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not proper for one while in the state of Ihram to use a thing which has saffron or any other kind of perfumes.’”
